title: Cross-chain borrow does not re-validate collateral at execution on destination chain
reason: Duplicate of existing F-002 (stale collateral snapshot / TOCTOU).

title: Cross-chain liquidation validates health AFTER message sent, causing stale execution
reason: Not a distinct new root cause; stale/liquidation-data issues are already captured (F-013/F-018), and the write-up misstates current execution checks.

title: Same-chain borrow calculation rounds to zero enabling collateral bypass
reason: Insufficient code support for the claimed rounding-to-zero path; major borrow bypass already captured by F-001.

title: Full repayment clears borrow records before verifying repay success
reason: Factually incorrect ordering: external repay is required first (`repayBorrow` at line 490), then storage removal occurs.

title: Supply uses stale exchange rate for lToken calculation allowing underminting
reason: Duplicate root cause of existing F-008 (stale pre-mint exchange rate accounting mismatch).

title: Cross-chain liquidation doesn't verify collateral chain liquidity before sending
reason: Claim unsupported: seize path updates internal accounting, not collateral-chain token cash transfers requiring liquidity precheck.

title: borrowForCrossChain missing zero address validation for borrower
reason: Low-value/unreachable in normal trusted-message flow; borrower is sourced from initiating user address, not arbitrary zero address.

title: repayBorrow accepts any lToken without underlying existence validation
reason: Unsupported-loss claim: invalid token address paths revert during SafeERC20 call execution rather than silently losing funds.

title: LiquidateSeizeUpdate doesn't return actual seized amount to liquidator
reason: Not a security vulnerability; observability/API ergonomics only.

title: Constructors accept any storage/oracle without contract validation
reason: Deployment-time trust/admin configuration concern, not a protocol runtime exploit under normal governance assumptions.

title: Cross-chain liquidation message can be built with zero destination lToken and guaranteed fail on receiver
reason: Primarily owner/mapping misconfiguration hardening issue; lacks a strong permissionless exploit path beyond self-failing operations.
